message:
refactor(oracles): Replace actor-leak baseline with mailbox-capacity and add invariant_from_violation helper

Meta-testing mutation infrastructure improvements:

- Replace baseline_actor_leak/mutation_actor_leak pair with mailbox-capacity
  tests that exercise bounded mailbox overflow detection via the mailbox
  oracle's configure_mailbox/on_send interface

- Add public invariant_from_violation() function that maps OracleViolation
  variants to their canonical invariant name constants, enabling external
  tooling and diagnostics to reference violations by human-readable name

Three-lane scheduler test fixes:

- Update waker construction in test to use WorkerCoordinator pattern instead
  of direct Parker reference, aligning with the production code's coordinator-
  based wake distribution model

- Fix next_wake counter assertions to read from scheduler.coordinator.next_wake
  rather than the now-removed top-level field, ensuring tests verify the
  correct round-robin worker notification behavior

Trace divergence import fix:

- Add missing CompactRegionId and CompactTaskId imports to divergence module
  tests, resolving compilation errors in the test suite

Proptest regressions:

- Preserve discovered shrunk failure case for property_cancellation test
  (phase1=Requested, phase2=Requested, kind1=RaceLost, kind2=FailFast, epoch=0)
  to ensure this edge case is always re-tested on future runs

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Seeds for failure cases proptest has generated in the past. It is
+# automatically read and these particular cases re-run before any
+# novel cases are generated.
+#
+# It is recommended to check this file in to source control so that
+# everyone who runs the test benefits from these saved cases.
+cc 5f4dc1752b7284c068a9873aa705c9a79798b928b08787db748a723b60072622 # shrinks to (phase1, phase2) = (Requested, Requested), kind1 = RaceLost, kind2 = FailFast, epoch = 0
